---
title: "Omics_data_summary"
author: "Savanah Leidholt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#directores
user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]

if (user == "leidholt") {
  root_path <- c("/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/")
  git_path <- "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/CHCO-Code/"
  keys <- fromJSON("/mmfs1/home/leidholt/keys.json")
} else if (user == "sleidholt") {
    root_path <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/sleidholt/Documents/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
    keys <- fromJSON("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Personal_storage/keys.json")
} else if (user == "savanahleidholt") {
    root_path <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/savanahleidholt/Desktop/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
    keys <- fromJSON("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Personal_storage/keys.json")
} else if (user == "choiyej") {
  root_path <- "/Users/choiyej/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UW/Bjornstad/Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/"
  git_path <- "/Users/choiyej/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad/"
  keys <- fromJSON("/Users/choiyej/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TheUniversityofColoradoDenver/Bjornstad Pyle Lab/keys.json")
} else if (user == "yejichoi") {
  root_path <- "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/yejichoi/"
  git_path <- "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/yejichoi/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
  keys <- fromJSON("/mmfs1/home/yejichoi/keys.json")
} else if (user == "pylell") {
  root_path <- "/Users/pylell/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-SharedLibraries-UW/Bjornstad/Biostatistics Core Shared Drive"
  git_path <- "/Users/pylell/Documents/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
} else if (user == "hhampson") {
  root_path <- "/Volumes/Peds Endo"
} else if (user == "sleidholt") {
  root_path <- "/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/"
  git_path <- "/Users/sleidholt/Documents/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad/T1D Adipostiy/"
} else {
  stop("Unknown user: please specify root path for this user.")
}
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(arsenal)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggpubr)
library(Hmisc)
library(readxl)
library(purrr)
library(aws.s3)
library(jsonlite)
library(kableExtra)


# Load your saved objects
omics_objects <- readRDS(file.path(root_path, "Savanah Leidholt", "T1D-T2D", "Data", "omics_split_objects_clean.rds"))

list2env(omics_objects, envir = .GlobalEnv)
```

```{r}
## Create an S3 client
Sys.setenv(
  "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID" = keys$MY_ACCESS_KEY,
  "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY" = keys$MY_SECRET_KEY,
  "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION" = "",
  "AWS_REGION" = "",
  "AWS_S3_ENDPOINT" = "s3.kopah.uw.edu"
)


pb90_multiomics_subset <- s3readRDS(
          object = "data_clean/pb90_multiomics_subset_meta_savanah_20260420.rds", 
          bucket = "scrna",
          region = "")
```

```{r}
# HELPER: count non-NA samples in a matrix for a given metadata
count_samples_with_data <- function(meta_df, mat_df) {
  # A sample "has data" if it has at least one non-NA value in the omics matrix
  has_data <- rowSums(!is.na(mat_df)) > 0
  meta_df %>%
    mutate(has_omics = has_data) %>%
    filter(has_omics)
}
```

# -----------------------------
# IDs with each data type
# -----------------------------
```{r}
ids_metab <- rownames(metabolites)[rowSums(!is.na(metabolites)) > 0]
ids_prot  <- rownames(prot_plasma)[rowSums(!is.na(prot_plasma)) > 0]
ids_scrna <- pb90_multiomics_subset %>%
  dplyr::pull(record_id) %>%
  unique()

# clean IDs
ids_metab <- trimws(as.character(ids_metab))
ids_prot  <- trimws(as.character(ids_prot))
ids_scrna <- trimws(as.character(ids_scrna))
ids_scrna
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Build clean metadata for each data type
# ============================================================

meta_metab_clean <- meta_metab %>%
  mutate(record_id = trimws(as.character(record_id))) %>%
  filter(record_id %in% ids_metab) %>%
  mutate(data_source = "Metabolomics")

meta_prot_clean <- meta_prot %>%
  mutate(record_id = trimws(as.character(record_id))) %>%
  filter(record_id %in% ids_prot) %>%
  mutate(data_source = "Proteomics")

meta_scrna_clean <- pb90_multiomics_subset %>%
  mutate(record_id = trimws(as.character(record_id))) %>%
  filter(record_id %in% ids_scrna) %>%
  distinct(record_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(data_source = "scRNA-seq")

# ============================================================
# Participant-level metadata with flags
# ============================================================

meta_all <- bind_rows(
  meta_prot_clean,
  meta_metab_clean,
  meta_scrna_clean
) %>%
  arrange(record_id, data_source) %>%
  distinct(record_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    has_metabolomics = record_id %in% ids_metab,
    has_proteomics   = record_id %in% ids_prot,
    has_scrna        = record_id %in% ids_scrna,
    has_all_three    = has_metabolomics & has_proteomics & has_scrna
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    age          = as.numeric(age),
    bmi          = as.numeric(bmi),
    hba1c        = as.numeric(hba1c),
    acr_u        = as.numeric(acr_u),
    eGFR_U25     = as.numeric(eGFR_CKiD_U25_avg),
    eGFR_fas_cr  = as.numeric(eGFR_fas_cr),
    sex          = as.factor(sex),
    group        = as.factor(group),
    study        = as.factor(study)
  )
```

```{r}
meta_all <- meta_all %>%
  mutate(
    obese = bmi >= 30
  )
```

```{r}
table(meta_all$group, meta_all$obese, useNA = "ifany")
```
```{r}
meta_all2 <- meta_all %>%
  filter(!(group == "Type 1 Diabetes" & obese))
```

```{r}
meta_metab_sub <- meta_all2 %>% filter(has_metabolomics)
meta_prot_sub  <- meta_all2 %>% filter(has_proteomics)
meta_scrna_sub <- meta_all2 %>% filter(has_scrna)
meta_all3_sub  <- meta_all2 %>% filter(has_all_three)
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# Helper functions for summary formatting
# ============================================================

mean_sd <- function(x, digits = 1) {
  if (all(is.na(x))) return("")
  sprintf(
    paste0("%.", digits, "f ± %.", digits, "f"),
    mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

median_iqr <- function(x, digits = 1) {
  if (all(is.na(x))) return("")
  q <- quantile(x, probs = c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
  sprintf(
    paste0("%.", digits, "f (%.", digits, "f, %.", digits, "f)"),
    median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q[1],
    q[2]
  )
}

n_pct <- function(x, level) {
  n <- sum(x == level, na.rm = TRUE)
  denom <- sum(!is.na(x))
  if (denom == 0) return("")
  paste0(n, " (", round(100 * n / denom, 1), "%)")
}
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Summary table by study
# ============================================================

make_summary_by_study <- function(data) {
  
  data %>%
    group_by(study) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Age = mean_sd(age),
      Female = n_pct(sex, "Female"),
      BMI = mean_sd(bmi),
      HbA1c = mean_sd(hba1c),
      `uACR, median` = median_iqr(acr_u),
      eGFR_fas_cr = mean_sd(eGFR_fas_cr),
      eGFR_U25 = mean_sd(eGFR_U25),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Categorical breakdown table by study
# ============================================================

make_categorical_by_study <- function(data, var) {
  
  data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[var]])) %>%
    count(study, .data[[var]], name = "n") %>%
    group_by(study) %>%
    mutate(
      pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1),
      value = paste0(n, " (", pct, "%)")
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(study, !!sym(var), value) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = !!sym(var),
      values_from = value,
      values_fill = "0 (0%)"
    )
}
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Render function with kableExtra
# ============================================================

render_kable <- function(tbl, caption) {
  
  tbl %>%
    kbl(
      caption = caption,
      align = "c",
      booktabs = TRUE
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      full_width = FALSE,
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
    ) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Overall summary by study
# ============================================================

tbl_overall <- make_summary_by_study(meta_all)

render_kable(
  tbl_overall,
  caption = "Overall Participant Characteristics by Study"
)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Data-type-specific summary tables
# ============================================================

render_kable(
  make_summary_by_group(meta_prot_sub),
  caption = "Proteomics Participant Characteristics by Group"
)

render_kable(
  make_summary_by_group(meta_metab_sub),
  caption = "Metabolomics Participant Characteristics by Group"
)

render_kable(
  make_summary_by_group(meta_scrna_sub),
  caption = "Single-cell RNA-seq Participant Characteristics by Group"
)

render_kable(
  make_summary_by_group(meta_all3_sub),
  caption = "Participants with Metabolomics + Proteomics + scRNA-seq by Group"
)

```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Optional: data-type-specific group distributions
# ============================================================

render_kable(
  make_categorical_by_study(meta_metab_sub, "group"),
  caption = "Metabolomics Group Distribution by Study"
)

render_kable(
  make_categorical_by_study(meta_prot_sub, "group"),
  caption = "Proteomics Group Distribution by Study"
)

render_kable(
  make_categorical_by_study(meta_scrna_sub, "group"),
  caption = "Single-cell RNA-seq Group Distribution by Study"
)

render_kable(
  make_categorical_by_study(meta_all3_sub, "group"),
  caption = "All Three Data Types Group Distribution by Study"
)
```
